entries.description: Enumerated reader call sites of isCancelled() / wasRestored() across the extension source. Findings: (1) tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/src/utils/fileSystem.ts:276 — sole production caller of isCancelled() for bucketing-state derivation; (2) wasRestored() has zero production callers — referenced only by tests; (3) src/providers/ActionRegistry.ts:41 defines a local 'isCancelled = (s: SessionSet) => s.state === "cancelled"' that operates on the resolved SessionState string, NOT the file-presence predicate — lookalike, unrelated. Audit drove the Session 1 refactor scope: a single caller to migrate, two legacy predicates to keep exported for the fallback path + test scaffolding.

Added readCancellationState(sessionSetDir) to tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/src/utils/cancelLifecycle.ts. Returns one of 'cancelled' | 'restored' | 'active' | 'unknown' as discrete CancellationState type union (also exported). Resolution order matches spec verbatim: (a) state.status === 'cancelled' → 'cancelled'; (b) status is non-cancelled string AND RESTORED.md present → 'restored' (history-aware); (c) status non-cancelled, no RESTORED.md → 'active'; (d) no state file / unparseable / non-string status → 'unknown' (caller must fall back to legacy file-presence predicate). Function does NOT consult CANCELLED.md when status is non-cancelled — the H2 contract intentionally lets the state file win; stray markdown is operator-resolvable. JSDoc on the legacy isCancelled() / wasRestored() predicates rewritten to tag them as legacy-fallback-only with a pointer at readCancellationState() as the primary entry point.

Refactored fileSystem.ts:276 to call readCancellationState() first. Bucketing branch: 'cancelled' return → state = 'cancelled' (Set-035 new behavior); 'unknown' + isCancelled(dir) → state = 'cancelled' (legacy fallback for v1 / hand-edited / brand-new folders) and emits console.warn naming the directory and pointing at ensure_state_file for repair (diagnostic trail for a state-file write bug masking a real cancellation). Any other return falls through to the existing readStatus/isMidSetComplete ladder unchanged — no regression on in-progress/complete/not-started bucketing. Import line updated to bring in readCancellationState alongside the retained isCancelled.

Rewrote three sections of docs/session-state-schema.md: (1) status-table footnote on 'cancelled' now names status: 'cancelled' as the CANONICAL signal (Set 035 extending H2 from Set 033 S2); the CANCELLED.md marker named as an audit-history artifact; (2) full 'Cancel / restore' section rewritten state-file-first — describes the canonical reader contract, the markdown files as audit artifacts, the legacy-fallback path with console.warn for diagnostics, and the no-silent-override rule for stray CANCELLED.md alongside non-cancelled status; (3) 'Bucketing in the Session Sets Explorer (v3)' first bullet now reads 'status === cancelled → Cancelled (state file wins, Set 035)' with the legacy fallback noted via section link. Per-session cancellation reservation (sessions[].status = 'cancelled' tolerated on read but not written by v3 writers) preserved unchanged.

Added 10 new test cases to tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/src/test/suite/cancelLifecycle.test.ts under suite 'cancelLifecycle — readCancellationState (Set 035 state-file-first)': state-cancelled + no marker → 'cancelled' (new behavior); state-complete + stray CANCELLED.md → 'active' (state file wins; legacy predicate still reports true to document the asymmetry); state-complete + RESTORED.md → 'restored' (history-aware); state-in-progress no markers → 'active'; state file missing → 'unknown'; unparseable JSON → 'unknown'; status-missing v1 → 'unknown'; legacy fallback contract (state absent + CANCELLED.md → caller uses isCancelled); end-to-end cancel then readCancellationState → 'cancelled'; end-to-end cancel then restore → 'restored'. All 10 new tests passing. Full extension suite: 456 passing, 2 pre-existing unrelated failures (configEditor ViewColumn.One stub gap; notificationsSection 'wired in Set 026 Session 7' assertion). Typecheck (npx tsc --noEmit) clean.

Bundled per operator directive 2026-05-21: removed the two grey placeholder
${renderGaugeSvg('unknown', 'current', 0)}
elements from renderAccordionEmpty() in tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/src/providers/OrchestratorAccordion.ts. Empty-state now renders only the .acc-empty-cta line (preserved smart-CTA mechanism). Comment block rewritten to explain the removal in operator language ('read as data we don't have, more confusing than useful, 2026-05-21') rather than as structural cleanup. renderGaugeSvg function still used by renderAccordionLoaded — removal scoped to the empty-state branch only. Layer-3 Playwright assertion on .acc-empty-cta + 'No signal' text still applies (no test broken by the change). Operator subsequently flagged a follow-on concern about the 'install Claude Code hook' CTA copy itself being Claude-specific; that audit + refactor work was scaffolded into a new Set 036 Session 6 (totalSessions 6 → 7; release session renumbered to S7) rather than expanded inline here.

Extended Set 036 (chatSessionId + watcher-scope implementation) from 6 to 7 sessions per operator directive 2026-05-21. Inserted new Session 6 'Orchestrator-agnostic UI audit + empty-state refactor' between current S5 (Layer-3 + cross-tier docs) and current S6 (release). Release session renumbered to Session 7. Updated: docs/session-sets/036-.../session-state.json (totalSessions 7, sessions[5] inserted, sessions[6] renumbered); docs/session-sets/036-.../spec.md (totalSessions in YAML config block, 'What ships across the seven sessions' overview, all five existing 'Session N of 6' headings flipped to 'of 7', new 'Session 6 of 7' block inserted with three disposition options (a/b/c) for the CTA refactor, release-session heading and Progress keys + close_session ref renumbered to S7, R7 risk rewritten for seven sessions, Total estimated cost table extended with S6 + S7 entries totaling $0.33-$1.05, Marketplace publish discipline cross-ref updated to S7).

Round A verification via gemini-pro (gemini-2.5-pro tier 2) at $0.0161025 cost (12,810 input tokens + 9 output tokens; 23.2s elapsed). Bundle size 34,518 chars covering: new readCancellationState + updated JSDoc on legacy predicates, fileSystem.ts:276 caller wiring + legacy fallback console.warn, new 10-case test suite, three rewritten session-state-schema.md sections, OrchestratorAccordion.ts empty-state simplification, full Session 1 spec contract, full project-overview context. Verdict: VERIFIED, no must-fix issues. Cumulative Set 035 routed spend: $0.0161 of $0.50 forecast.
